title: Earth Sciences
description: A collection of RAND research on the topic of Earth Sciences
text:
Supporting Climate-Related Decisions Under Uncertainty
The chapter demonstrates an RDM approach to identifying a robust mix of policy instruments—carbon taxes and technology subsidies—for reducing greenhouse gas emissions.
Mid-Atlantic Regional Climate Impacts Summary and Outlook: Fall 2025
This climate summary aims to inform Mid-Atlantic policymakers and practitioners about weather patterns during the 2025 fall season, regional climate trends, and the upcoming winter season.
Tracking Multinational Corporate Climate Action from Space: A Measurement Framework for Evaluating Supply Chain Policies using Satellite Monitoring and Climate Disclosures
In this dissertation, the author analyzes how supply chain policies for climate change mitigation have manifested among multinational companies, how the policies’ effects have migrated across borders, and whether the policies are effective.
Planning for an Uncertain Future
RAND brings a wealth of knowledge in Decision Making under Deep Uncertainty, helping local, state, and federal leaders identify low-regret investments that can provide value regardless of how conditions evolve.
Mid-Atlantic Regional Climate Impacts Summary and Outlook: Summer 2025
This climate summary aims to inform Mid-Atlantic policymakers and practitioners about weather patterns during the 2025 summer season, regional climate trends, and the upcoming fall season.
Evaluation of the Centre for Earth Observation Instrumentation: Final report
CEOI is an important feature of the UK Earth Observation landscape, funding innovative projects which lead to enhanced technological progression and promising mission concepts. The programme is well liked by grant recipients, and is well run.
Evaluation of the Centre for Earth Observation Instrumentation (CEOI) Programme
RAND Europe, in partnership with know.space and two independent Earth Observation (EO) experts, were commissioned by the UK Space Agency (UKSA) to evaluate the Centre for Earth Observation Instrumentation programme.
Cities and Climate Change: Identifying, Measuring and Evaluating Public Policy for Heatwaves
The author of this dissertation addresses gaps in the literature by examining methods for measuring vulnerability to extreme heat events and evaluating policies to enhance urban resilience.
Applying the Orphan Drug Policy to America's Mining Industry
U.S. dependency on Chinese-sourced critical minerals poses a threat to national security. How can the United States stimulate critical mineral production? The Orphan Drug Act of 1983, the 2007 FDA Act, and the 2012 FDA Safety and Innovation Act may hold the answers.
Mid-Atlantic Regional Climate Impacts Summary and Outlook: Spring 2025
This climate summary aims to inform Mid-Atlantic policymakers and practitioners about weather patterns during the 2025 spring season, regional climate trends, and the upcoming summer season.
Is the Bar Higher for Scientific Claims of Alien Life?
Grandiose headlines often promise proof that we are not alone, but scientists remain cautious. Is this caution unique to the field of astrobiology? In truth, major scientific breakthroughs are rarely accepted quickly.
